# coreweave_sandbox_profile_template (Resource)

Manage CoreWeave Sandbox profile templates. A profile template is a reusable specification (image, resources, node placement, networking) that managed runners attach via `profile_bindings`.

The `description`, `spec`, and `labels` fields are mutable; changing `display_name` forces replacement.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "coreweave_sandbox_profile_template" "default" {
  display_name = "default-cpu"
  description  = "Default CPU profile for CI sandboxes."

  spec = {
    container_image = "ghcr.io/coreweave/sandbox-runtime:v1"
    runtime_class   = "kata-qemu"

    resource_defaults = {
      cpu_request    = "500m"
      memory_request = "1Gi"
      cpu_limit      = "2"
      memory_limit   = "4Gi"
    }

    instance_types = ["cpu.small", "cpu.medium"]
    tags           = ["ci"]

    node_selector = {
      "workload-class" = "general"
    }
  }

  labels = {
    team = "platform"
    env  = "prod"
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `display_name` (String) Human-readable template name. Must be unique within the organization. Changing this forces a replacement.
- `spec` (Attributes) Profile specification — compute, networking, and placement defaults applied to sandboxes that select this profile. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--spec))

### Optional

- `description` (String) Human-readable description.
- `labels` (Map of String) User-defined labels for organizing templates.

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (String) RFC3339 timestamp at which the template was created.
- `id` (String) Server-assigned UUID for the profile template.
- `organization_id` (String) Organization that owns this template.
- `updated_at` (String) RFC3339 timestamp at which the template was most recently updated.

<a id="nestedatt--spec"></a>
### Nested Schema for `spec`

Optional:

- `container_image` (String) Container image override (e.g., `ghcr.io/coreweave/sandbox-runtime:v1`).
- `instance_types` (List of String) Allowed instance types for sandboxes using this profile.
- `namespace_config_json` (String) Namespace strategy configuration. Must be canonical JSON; use `jsonencode({...})` to construct.
- `network_config_json` (String) Network configuration. Must be canonical JSON; use `jsonencode({...})` to construct.
- `node_selector` (Map of String) Node selector labels.
- `pod_template_json` (String) Pod template overrides as a JSON-encoded `PodSpec` fragment. Must be canonical JSON; use `jsonencode({...})` to construct.
- `resource_defaults` (Attributes) Default CPU and memory requests/limits applied to sandboxes that don't specify their own. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--spec--resource_defaults))
- `runtime_class` (String) Runtime class name (e.g., `kata-qemu`, `gvisor`) used for isolation.
- `tags` (List of String) Tags for RBAC-based access control.

<a id="nestedatt--spec--resource_defaults"></a>
### Nested Schema for `spec.resource_defaults`

Optional:

- `cpu_limit` (String) Default CPU limit (e.g., `2`).
- `cpu_request` (String) Default CPU request (e.g., `500m`).
- `memory_limit` (String) Default memory limit (e.g., `4Gi`).
- `memory_request` (String) Default memory request (e.g., `1Gi`).

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `id` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = coreweave_sandbox_profile_template.default
  id = "{{id}}"
}
```

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import coreweave_sandbox_profile_template.default {{id}}
```
